23.reason: [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers that remove `gets` entirely.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers that remove `gets` entirely.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using `gets()` is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments, `gets()` is removed entirely, leading to a compilation error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers due to removal from standard library]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library in C11; many compilers no longer support it, leading to a compilation error. Use 'fgets' instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using `gets()` is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments, `gets()` is not available, leading to a compilation error.]

49.reason: [The condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect because the array `num` is never initialized to -1; this leads to undefined behavior when shifting elements.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect because the array `num` is never initialized to -1; this leads to undefined behavior when shifting elements.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect because the array `num` is never initialized to -1. This leads to undefined behavior when shifting elements, possibly accessing beyond valid data or infinite loops. It should instead run until the end of the valid numbers (e.g., using a counter).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect because the array `num` is never initialized to -1; this leads to undefined behavior when shifting elements]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The condition 'num[m]!=-1' is incorrect because the array 'num' is never initialized with -1 as a sentinel. This leads to undefined behavior when shifting elements, and may cause out-of-bounds access or infinite loops. The loop should run until the end of valid numbers, which corresponds to the length of the 'op' array plus one.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect because the array `num` is never initialized with -1 as a sentinel. This leads to undefined behavior when accessing beyond valid data, possibly causing infinite loops or segmentation faults.]

62.reason: [Same issue: `num[m]!=-1` is invalid since `num` isn't filled with -1 as sentinel values.]
